title: feature request - dynamic injection of secrets
body:
## In what area(s)?

> /area runtime

## Describe the feature
Currently, Dapr requires a manual restart of the runtime sidecars or components to pick up updates when secrets are changed in an upstream secret store (e.g., Kubernetes Secrets, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault). This manual intervention increases operational overhead  during secret rotations.

I want Dapr to automatically monitor upstream secret stores for changes. Whether the upstream store supports push-based watches (like Kubernetes) or requires periodic polling (like Vault or AWS), Dapr should detect these updates and refresh its internal state in the runtime dynamically. This ensures that sensitive credentials and configurations stay current without requiring any manual restarts or Dapr instance downtime ensuring a smoother and more automated operational experience.


## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE: **ADD**  dynamic injection of secrets

comments.body:
👍🏻 Totally agree, this feature is a must-have.

We’re currently facing challenges around certificate rotation, and it would be extremely helpful if Dapr could support hot reloading. This would allow us to avoid pod rollouts, which is especially important when working with legacy StatefulSets that cannot be safely restarted during business hours.

Hi!

I'd like to have more details, as in my local tests using dapr v1.18.1 it's working fine.

I've setup an app that uses a redis statestore, and the password is coming from a secret. This is the component:
```yaml
apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: secret-statestore
spec:
  type: state.redis
  version: v1
  metadata:
  - name: redisHost
    value: secret-redis.default.svc.cluster.local:6379
  - name: redisPassword
    secretKeyRef:
      name: redis-secret
      key: redis-password
auth:
  secretStore: kubernetes
```

When I change the `redis-secret.redis-password` secret (`kubectl patch secret redis-secret -n default --type merge -p '{"stringData":{"redis-password":"wrong-password"}}'`), dapr detects the change (it's checking every 60 seconds by default), and reloads the component using the new password. Specifically, in my test I set the password to a wrong one, and the statestore stopped working within 60 seconds without any need of reloading the app. Then I've set it to the right one, and the component started working again within 60 seconds.

This should work for any kind of secret store.

Can you show me your usecase? I will check why it doesn't work in your case.
